authority_boundary.allowed_claim: Public target-shape route cases can admit only tactics that were both probed and available before proof execution.
forbidden_claims: the selected tactic proves the goal
Lean/Lake or Mathlib-dependent proof execution is authorized
proof bodies or post-oracle execution labels may be exposed

validation_rules: Every allowed tactic id must exist in the public tactic portfolio.
Every allowed tactic id must be marked available before proof execution.
Every route case must be pre_execution.
Every required Ring2 target-shape source artifact must be copied into source_artifacts under the fixture or bundle input.
Every copied Ring2 target-shape source artifact sha256 digest must match source_digests.
Proof, provider, and Lean source body fields are forbidden.
Release, provider, proof-authority, and Lean/Lake authority overclaims are rejected.
receipt_expectations: result receipt with scored route decisions
board receipt with public contract and routing projection
source artifact import rows with copied-body and digest-match status
source_module_manifest.json with copied Ring2 source-body rows, source refs, target refs, sha256, line_count, material_class, and body_in_receipt=false

standard_payload.ontology: target-shape tactic routing is a pre-execution Ring2 route gate backed by copied non-secret macro run artifacts plus public fixture decisions
standard_payload.teleology: raise the body floor by making the actual Ring2 target-shape run summaries, failure taxonomy, and graph-update candidates present and digest-checked in Microcosm before routing claims are accepted
standard_payload.anti_purpose: do not treat copied route artifacts as theorem proof authority, provider payload authority, release authority, or private macro equivalence
compression_bands: source_artifacts are copied body evidence and must be digest-checked
route cases are public projections over the copied Ring2 source bodies
receipts may name source artifacts and digests but must not echo proof/provider/private bodies
source_module_manifest and source_open_body_imports make copied target-shape Ring2 bodies countable by status and workingness without exporting body text in receipts
failure_modes: copied Ring2 target-shape source artifact is missing from the public fixture or bundle
copied Ring2 target-shape source artifact digest differs from the macro source digest
route admits a tactic absent from the public probe portfolio
route admits a tactic marked unavailable by the public probe
route case is selected after Lean/proof execution evidence exists
proof body, provider payload, or Lean source body appears in public output
graph-update candidate is treated as theorem proof authority
release, provider, or Lean execution authority is implied by pre-execution routing metadata

anti_claim: This standard governs real Ring2 target-shape routing refs only; it does not certify theorem correctness, tactic success, provider output, release readiness, or private macro equivalence.
